title: Hieracium subvaldepilosum
taxonomy: Hieracium subvaldepilosum belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Hieracium, which includes over 1,000 species of hawkweeds. The genus Hieracium is typically classified as being in the subfamily Cichorioideae and tribe Senecioneae. This classification helps to group Hieracium subvaldepilosum with other plants that share similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Further classification would include its kingdom (Plantae) and other taxonomic ranks.

intro: Hieracium subvaldepilosum, also known as a small-leaved hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, anti-inflammatory properties, and antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable remedy in folk medicine. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium subvaldepilosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly prepared to address digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory ailments.
